articles.title: Trump Delays Threat to Iran, but War Negotiations Are in Early Stage
articles.snippet: President Trump postponed his threat to strike power plants in Iran, citing "productive conversations" with the Iranians. But officials said the talks were in an early stage and not substantive.

articles.title: Colombian Military Plane Crash Kills at Least 66
articles.snippet: Colombia's defence minister says it was a "tragic accident" but the cause is not yet clear. A military cargo plane with 128 people on board crashed shortly after taking off in southwestern Colombia.

articles.title: In LaGuardia Crash That Killed 2, Call to 'Stop!' Came Too Late
articles.snippet: A collision between an Air Canada Express jet and a fire truck on Sunday night left two pilots dead and dozens injured. The air traffic controller reportedly said he 'messed up'.

articles.title: Oil Back Above $100 as Conflicting Claims Emerge on US-Iran Talks
articles.snippet: Global energy prices plunged on Monday after Trump said he had postponed strikes on Iranian power plants, but oil has rebounded amid uncertainty over negotiations.

articles.title: Australia and EU Agree Sweeping Trade Deal in Face of Global Uncertainty
articles.snippet: Australia and the EU sign sweeping trade and security deals after years of negotiations.

articles.title: Markwayne Mullin Confirmed as Homeland Security Secretary
articles.snippet: A Cherokee Nation member who has served as Oklahoma's junior senator, Mr. Mullin will take charge at a pivotal time.

articles.title: Denmark Holds Early Elections Spurred by Trump's Threats to Take Greenland
articles.snippet: Denmark's prime minister called early parliamentary elections after gaining a popularity boost from standing up to President Trump over his threat to seize Greenland.

articles.title: Airstrikes May Have Destroyed Iran's Last F-14s, Ending a Long, Strange Saga
articles.snippet: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

articles.title: All New Homes to Have Heat Pumps and Solar Panels
articles.snippet: Solar panels that can be plugged in at home could be available to buy in supermarkets in the coming months.

articles.title: ICE Deployments Created Chaos for Cities and Cost Them Millions, NPR Analysis Finds
articles.snippet: Local leaders report already-strapped police departments racked up overtime bills in the millions while others report a multi-million dollar hit to business during the worst ICE surges.
